channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame induction ceremony has a poignant vibe this year
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The Rock & Roll Hall Fame induction ceremony on Saturday night promises to be starry, jamming — and bittersweet. So many of the honorees this time have been lost. Of the seven original members of Kool & the Gang, there is only one, Robert “Kool” Bell.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judges punishing Jan. 6 rioters say they fear more political violence as Election Day nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Over the past four years, judges at Washington’s federal courthouse have punished hundreds of rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol in an unprecedented assault on the nation's democracy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mail carriers reach tentative contract with USPS that includes pay raises and air-conditioned trucks
item.description: Some 200,000 letter carriers have reached a tentative contract deal with the U.S. Postal Service that includes backdated pay raises and a promise to provide workers with air-conditioned trucks.
item.dc:creator: David Sharp And Nick Perry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Where is the 'best restroom' in the U.S.? In a Utah gas station
item.description: The runners-up of this year's contest included a disco party bathroom, a bathroom full of mirrors and neon lights, and a very reflective 80s-inspired restroom in a brunch restaurant, among others.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KRON4

item.title: Stanford psychologist behind the controversial "Stanford Prison Experiment" dies at 91
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Philip G. Zimbardo, the psychologist behind the controversial “Stanford Prison Experiment” that was intended to examine the psychological experiences of imprisonment, has died. He was 91. Stanford University announced Friday that Zimbardo died Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Guatemalan judge grants investigative journalist Zamora house arrest and his family celebrates
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — The family of a Guatemalan journalist on Saturday celebrated his transfer to house arrest following his jailing for more than two years amid his daily newspaper's anti-corruption investigations.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PEREZ D., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Two Texas cities represent the divide between those who vote and those who could, but often don't
item.description: LEWISVILLE, Texas (AP) — Deep in the heart of Texas' sprawl, the city of Lewisville embodies the Lone Star State.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With brain injuries a growing problem, the US military tests how to protect troops from blasts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The blast shook the ground and its red flash of fire covered the doorway as U.S. special operations forces blew open a door during a recent training exercise.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Your Morning: 50 dead in Gaza as Israel and Hamas trade strikes
item.description: Welcome to the Your Morning newsletter, featuring the latest news across America! It’s Saturday, Oct. 19, 2024, and there’s a lot happening.
item.dc:creator: Scott Fagerstrom, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia parents charged with child cruelty and false imprisonment of 4 teenagers
item.description: Two Georgia parents are facing charges over accusations of child cruelty and false imprisonment of their four teenagers who were living under conditions described as inhumane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yankees get to Guardians closer Clase again, score 2 runs in 9th to win 8-6 and take 3-1 ALCS lead
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Cleveland All-Star closer Emmanuel Clase gave up two runs in the ninth inning and the New York Yankees moved closer to another World Series, beating the Guardians 8-6 on Friday night to take a 3-1 lead in the American League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack, report finds
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WGN 9

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jury paves the way for 2 Live Crew to retake control of records that changed hip-hop
item.description: Miami's 2 Live Crew helped redraw the legal landscape around what hip-hop could be, pushing the boundaries of free speech and taste with their provocative and sexually explicit recordings that led to landmark court decisions protecting the rights of artists.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Buy a tiny house at Walmart? You can for under $16K
item.description: While there are dozens of things to consider before purchasing a tiny home, it's never been easier to find and buy one.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 20,000 people are missing in Baja California, group says
item.description: "We don't believe every missing person is a victim of something malicious or a crime, maybe they fell to addiction or have no way of communicating with anyone from the streets, that's why we have to go looking.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Would-be Trump assassin wants Judge Cannon to recuse herself for dismissing document case
item.description: Ryan Routh, who is accused of an assassination attempt on former President Donald Trump, has demanded that the Trump-appointed federal judge presiding over his case be recused.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rape suspect admits to faking death, lying about identity
item.description: Nicholas Alahverdian admitted Wednesday he faked his death and lied about his identity before fleeing to Europe.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Texas doctor accused of illegally providing transgender hormone treatments to minors
item.description: A Texas doctor faces a lawsuit from the state after allegedly prescribing transgender hormone treatments to over 20 biological females in violation of a Republican-backed ban.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Nevada Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.) and Republican Sam Brown duked it out during their first and only debate Thursday night, making their pitches to voters in one of the key battleground states on the 2024 map and a must-win state for Democratic Senate majority hopes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Helene 'like a bomb went off': Florida Rep Kat Cammack details severe hurricane damage
item.description: Florida Rep. Kat Cammack said that her district has suffered significant damage from Hurricane Helene, including "astronomical" agricultural damage and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, ABC 4

item.title: Texas Supreme Court halts execution of man in shaken baby case after lawmakers' last-minute appeal
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — The Texas Supreme Court halted Thursday night’s scheduled execution of a man who would have become the first person in the U.S. put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli forces in Gaza killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year’s attack on Israel that sparked the war, the military said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Alabama executes man who killed 5 and asked to be put to death
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — Alabama executed a man Thursday who admitted to killing five people with an ax and gun during a drug-fueled rampage in 2016 and dropped his appeals and asked to be put to death. Derrick Dearman, 36, was pronounced dead at 6:14 p.m. Thursday at Holman prison in southern Alabama.
item.dc:creator: BY KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Nevada US Senate Debate: Jacky Rosen and Sam Brown
item.description: As the seconds tick away until Election Day on Nov. 5, Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown are set to debate the issues Nevadans are most concerned about heading into 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Menendez brothers' uncle requests DA meeting; says boys' motive was greed
item.description: Anderson believes Erik and Lyle Menendez weren't molested by their father Jose and that "justice was served.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, WGN 9

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) privately slammed former President Donald Trump as “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and “a despicable human being” after the 2020 election, according to a biography of the senator set to be released later this month.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: 3 dead, several injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and several others were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, ABC 4

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5
